quotes.text: Productive work is the central purpose of a rational man's life, the central value that integrates and determines the hierarchy of all his other values. Reason is the source, the precondition of his productive work — pride is the result.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics" — The Virtue of Selfishness

quotes.text: Man's mind is his basic tool of survival. Life is given to him, survival is not. His body is given to him, its sustenance is not. His mind is given to him, its content is not. To remain alive, he must act, and before he can act he must know the nature and purpose of his action.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: The faculty that distinguishes man from animals is reason. The function of reason is to perceive the reality which exists and to integrate it into knowledge.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "For the New Intellectual"

quotes.text: A is A. A thing is itself. You will not assault reality without paying the price, and the price is your own destruction.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: Contradictions do not exist. Whenever you think that you are facing a contradiction, check your premises. You will find that one of them is wrong.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: Reason is not automatic. Those who deny it cannot be conquered by it. Do not count on them. Leave them alone.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: Thinking is man's only basic virtue, from which all the others proceed. The virtue of Rationality means the recognition and acceptance of reason as one's only source of knowledge, one's only judge of values and one's only guide to action.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics"

quotes.text: The pursuit of a rational purpose is the only form of pleasure that does not corrupt or deplete the man who experiences it.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: An emotion that clashes with your reason, an emotion that you cannot explain or control, is only the carcass of that stale thinking which you forbade your mind to revise.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: Happiness is that state of consciousness which proceeds from the achievement of one's values.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics"

quotes.text: To live, man must hold three things as the supreme and ruling values of his life: Reason — Purpose — Self-esteem.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics"

quotes.text: The hardest thing to explain is the glaringly evident which everybody had decided not to see.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: Throughout the centuries there were men who took first steps down new roads armed with nothing but their own vision.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead — Roark's courtroom speech

quotes.text: The creator stands on his own judgment. The parasite follows the opinions of others. The creator thinks, the parasite copies.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead — Roark's courtroom speech

quotes.text: I came here to say that I do not recognize anyone's right to one minute of my life. Nor to any part of my energy. Nor to any achievement of mine. No matter who makes the claim, how large their number or how great their need.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead — Roark's courtroom speech

quotes.text: I wished to come here and say that I am a man who does not exist for others. It had to be said. The world is perishing from an orgy of self-sacrificing.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead — Roark's courtroom speech

quotes.text: The mind is an attribute of the individual. There is no such thing as a collective brain. There is no such thing as a collective thought.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead — Roark's courtroom speech

quotes.text: No man can use his brain to think for another. All the functions of body and spirit are private. They cannot be shared or transferred.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead — Roark's courtroom speech

quotes.text: I swear by my life and my love of it that I will never live for the sake of another man, nor ask another man to live for mine.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's oath

quotes.text: Independence is the recognition of the fact that yours is the responsibility of judgment and nothing can help you escape it.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics"

quotes.text: No substitute can do your thinking, as no pinch-hitter can live your life.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics"

quotes.text: A first-handed ego is the one who reverses convention. The first questions he asks of his work are never "What will they think of it?" The first questions are "Is it true? Is it right? Does it work?"
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead

quotes.text: Howard, the worst second-hander of all — the man who goes after power.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead — Dominique to Roark

quotes.text: Civilization is the progress toward a society of privacy. The savage's whole existence is public, ruled by the laws of his tribe. Civilization is the process of setting man free from men.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead

quotes.text: Achievement of your happiness is the only moral purpose of your life, and that happiness — not pain or mindless self-indulgence — is the proof of your moral integrity.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: The question isn't who is going to let me; it's who is going to stop me.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead

quotes.text: To say "I love you" one must know first how to say the "I."
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead

quotes.text: I never thought of him in relation to others. I thought of him in relation to himself.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead

quotes.text: Do not let your fire go out, spark by irreplaceable spark, in the hopeless swamps of the approximate, the not-quite, the not-yet, the not-at-all.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: Honesty is the recognition of the fact that the unreal is unreal and can have no value, that neither love nor fame nor cash is a value if obtained by fraud.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics"

quotes.text: The first right on earth is the right of the ego. Man's first duty is to himself. His moral law is never to place his prime goal within the persons of others.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead — Roark's courtroom speech

quotes.text: An honest man is one who knows that he can't consume more than he has produced.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — Francisco's money speech

quotes.text: Money will not give him a code of values, if he's evaded the knowledge of what to value, and it will not provide him with a purpose, if he's evaded the choice of what to seek.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — Francisco's money speech

quotes.text: The unfocused mind is not conscious. The man who lives without consciousness, with thoughts unowned and acts unconsidered, has not earned the right to call himself human.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: Evasion is the act of blanking out, the willful refusal to see — the willful refusal to be conscious.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: You cannot stand watching the dishonesty around you and decide to do nothing about your own integrity — for integrity is single, you cannot have it in one area of your life and lose it in another.
quotes.source: Leonard Peikoff, Objectivism: The Philosophy of Ayn Rand

quotes.text: The man who attempts to live for the sake of others is a dependent. He is a parasite in motive and makes parasites of those he serves.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead — Roark's courtroom speech

quotes.text: Justice is the recognition of the fact that you cannot fake the character of men as you cannot fake the character of nature, that you must judge all men as conscientiously as you judge inanimate objects, with the same respect for truth.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: The only way men can deal with one another is by trade, and the only proper criterion of trade is justice: a trade by free, mutual consent to mutual advantage.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics"

quotes.text: When you condemn the guilty, you uphold the innocent. When you let the guilty pass, you condemn the innocent.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: The man who refuses to judge, who neither agrees nor disagrees, who declares that there are no absolutes and believes that he escapes responsibility, is the man responsible for all the blood that is now spilled in the world.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: There are two sides to every issue: one side is right and the other is wrong, but the middle is always evil.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: Force and mind are opposites; morality ends where a gun begins.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: Whoever is now within reach of my voice, whoever is man the victim, not man the killer — I am speaking at the deathbed of your mind, at the brink of that darkness in which you're drowning, and if you have not yet decided to relinquish your hold on the world, hear me.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: Discard the protective rags of that vice which you called a virtue: humility — learn to value yourself, which means: to fight for your happiness.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: The proper method of judging when or whether one should help another person is by reference to one's own rational self-interest and one's own hierarchy of values: the time, money, or effort one gives or the risk one takes should be proportionate to the value of the person in relation to one's own happiness.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Ethics of Emergencies" — The Virtue of Selfishness

quotes.text: A creative man is motivated by the desire to achieve, not by the desire to beat others.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead

quotes.text: Wealth is the product of man's capacity to think.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — Francisco's money speech

quotes.text: Run for your life from any man who tells you that money is evil. That sentence is the leper's bell of an approaching looter.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — Francisco's money speech

quotes.text: If you don't know, the thing to do is not to get scared but to learn.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: The smallest minority on earth is the individual. Those who deny individual rights cannot claim to be defenders of minorities.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Roots of War" — Capitalism: The Unknown Ideal

quotes.text: Every aspect of Western culture needs a new code of ethics — a rational ethics — as a precondition of rebirth.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "For the New Intellectual"

quotes.text: Civilization is the progress toward a society of privacy.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead

quotes.text: The man who lets a leader prescribe his course is a wreck being towed to the scrap heap.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead

quotes.text: Anyone who fights for the future, lives in it today.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, journal entry, 1945

quotes.text: Pride is the recognition of the fact that you are your own highest value and, like all of man's values, it has to be earned.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics"

quotes.text: The virtue of Pride is moral ambitiousness — the dedication to achieve one's own moral perfection.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics"

quotes.text: Man cannot survive except by gaining knowledge, and reason is his only means to gain it. Reason is the faculty that perceives, identifies and integrates the material provided by his senses.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "Faith and Force: The Destroyers of the Modern World"

quotes.text: Man has to be man — by choice; he has to hold his life as a value — by choice; he has to learn to sustain it — by choice; he has to discover the values it requires and practice his virtues — by choice.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics"

quotes.text: There can be no compromise between a property owner and a burglar. Whatever the burglar gains, the owner loses, no matter what excuses or rationalizations are offered.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "Doesn't Life Require Compromise?" — The Virtue of Selfishness

quotes.text: A morality of rational selfishness is a morality of life, of consciousness, of love of one's own values and of refusal to substitute the values of others.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, The Virtue of Selfishness

quotes.text: Sweep aside those parasites of subsidized classrooms, who live on the profits of the mind of others and proclaim that man needs no morality, no values, no code of behavior.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: It is not advisable, James, to venture unsolicited opinions. You should spare yourself the embarrassing discovery of their exact value to your listener.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — Francisco

quotes.text: Throughout the centuries, the man who placed his work above himself, who lived for the joy of his work — was the only kind of being who never sacrificed others to his pleasure.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead

quotes.text: When a creator dies, he doesn't die — his ideas, his vision, his work live on and create the world he wanted.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead

quotes.text: Reason demands the long-range view. Reason is a faculty that acts on principle.
quotes.source: Leonard Peikoff, Objectivism: The Philosophy of Ayn Rand

quotes.text: To live for his own sake means that the achievement of his own happiness is man's highest moral purpose.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics"

quotes.text: Man's basic vice, the source of all his evils, is the act of unfocusing his mind, the suspension of his consciousness, which is not blindness, but the refusal to see, not ignorance, but the refusal to know.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: Honors and praise from those of inferior judgment carry the contagion of their fault.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead

quotes.text: The smallest, most personal, most private of decisions — what to think, what to value, what to do — is the one that no other man can make for you.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: Productiveness is your acceptance of morality, your recognition of the fact that you choose to live — that productive work is the process by which man's consciousness controls his existence.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: Your work is the process of achieving your values. To lose your ambition for values is to lose your ambition to live.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: The man who does not value himself, cannot value anything or anyone.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: Reality is that which exists; the unreal does not exist; the unreal is merely that negation of existence which is the content of a human consciousness when it attempts to abandon reason.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: The only proof of one's mind is the unbreached objectivity with which one views and judges all matters.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Leonard Peikoff, OPAR

quotes.text: The pursuit of truth is the only road to happiness on earth.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: A wish is not the equivalent of an act. The wish to do is not the doing.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: To deal with men by force is as impractical as to deal with nature by persuasion.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Nature of Government" — The Virtue of Selfishness

quotes.text: A man who cannot acknowledge the man he is, denies the right of existence to the man he could become.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, journals

quotes.text: The hardest words to utter in any language are: I was wrong. They are also the most liberating.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Leonard Peikoff, OPAR

quotes.text: A standard is an abstract principle that serves as a measurement or gauge to guide a man's choices in the achievement of a concrete, specific purpose.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, "The Objectivist Ethics"

quotes.text: The good, says the altruist, is the welfare of others; man, the man who needs the good, is the means by which it is achieved. This is the doctrine of human sacrifice.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: There are no contradictions in reality; only in human thinking.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: An emotion as such tells you nothing about reality, beyond the fact that something makes you feel something.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Philosophy: Who Needs It

quotes.text: A philosophical system is an integrated view of existence. As a human being, you have no choice about the fact that you need a philosophy. Your only choice is whether you define your philosophy by a conscious, rational, disciplined process of thought.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Philosophy: Who Needs It

quotes.text: The man of self-esteem knows it is impossible to fake his own consciousness; he can fake reality for a moment, but not his own awareness of having faked it.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, The Virtue of Selfishness

quotes.text: The man who lies to the world, is the world's slave from then on.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, journals

quotes.text: A man who tells you that he has a special way of knowing reality, by-passing reason, is a man asking you to surrender your mind.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, Philosophy: Who Needs It

quotes.text: Productiveness is the road of unlimited achievement and calls upon the highest attributes of your character.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech

quotes.text: Pride is the sum of all virtues.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: To work — only to feed the consumption of others; to live — only on borrowed values; this is not life, it is a slow extinction.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: Suffering is not a value, only man's fight against suffering is. If you have not chosen your happiness as the highest purpose, you have chosen suffering.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged

quotes.text: A culture, no less than a person, lives by its values.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Philosophy: Who Needs It

quotes.text: It does not matter who first thought of an idea; what matters is whether it is true.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Leonard Peikoff, OPAR

quotes.text: Selfishness is a magnificent force — provided your self is something worth being.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, journals

quotes.text: To say "I want it" is not enough. You must be willing to do what reality requires to get it.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, The Romantic Manifesto

quotes.text: You can avoid reality, but you cannot avoid the consequences of avoiding reality.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: Self-esteem is reliance on one's power to think. It cannot be replaced by one's power to deceive.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Ayn Rand, The Virtue of Selfishness

quotes.text: The choice to live is the choice to think. To refuse to think is to refuse to live as a human being.
quotes.source: Paraphrased from Leonard Peikoff, OPAR

quotes.text: Do not say that you're afraid to trust your mind because you know so little. Are you safer in surrendering to mystics and discarding the little that you know? Live and act within the limit of your knowledge and keep expanding it to the limit of your life.
quotes.source: Ayn Rand, Atlas Shrugged — John Galt's speech
